تَلْبَسُ هَنا حِذاءَها الرِّياضِيَّ الجَدِيدَ.
Hana puts on her new sports shoes.
تَجْرِي هَنا وَراءَ الكُرَةِ بِسُرْعَةٍ.
Hana runs after the ball quickly.
الفَرِيقُ الآخَرُ يُسَجِّلُ هَدَفاً. واحِد - صِفْر!
The other team scores a goal. One to zero!
تَقُولُ هَنا لِفَرِيقِها: هَيّا نُحاوِلُ مَعاً!
Hana says to her team: let's try together!
يَلْعَبُ الفَرِيقُ مَعاً وَيُمَرِّرُ الكُرَةَ.
The team plays together and passes the ball.
تُسَجِّلُ هَنا هَدَفاً جَمِيلاً! واحِد - واحِد!
Hana scores a beautiful goal! One to one!
في النِّهايَةِ، تُسَجِّلُ لَيْلَى هَدَفَ الفَوْزِ!
At the end, Layla scores the winning goal!
يَفْرَحُ الفَرِيقُ. اللَّعِبُ مَعاً أَجْمَلُ شَيْءٍ!
The team celebrates. Playing together is the best thing!
What was the score after the other team scored?
The other team scored first, so it was one to zero.
What did Hana tell her team?
Hana told her team to keep trying together.
Who scored the winning goal?
Layla scored the winning goal at the end.
The Ramadan Lantern. Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.
يَبْدَأُ شَهْرُ رَمَضانَ اللَّيْلَةَ.
The month of Ramadan begins tonight.
تُرِيدُ هَنا فانُوساً جَمِيلاً مِثْلَ كُلِّ سَنَةٍ.
Hana wants a beautiful lantern, like every year.
تَذْهَبُ هَنا مَعَ بابا إِلَى السُّوقِ.
Hana goes with Baba to the market.
الفَوانِيسُ كُلُّها مُباعَةٌ! هَنا حَزِينَةٌ.
All the lanterns are sold! Hana is sad.
يَقُولُ جِدُّو: نَصْنَعُ فانُوساً بِأَيْدِينا!
Geddo says: we will make a lantern with our own hands!
تَقُصُّ هَنا وَرَقاً أَحْمَرَ وَأَصْفَرَ.
Hana cuts red and yellow paper.
يَضَعُ جِدُّو مِصْباحاً صَغِيراً في الفانُوسِ.
Geddo puts a little light inside the lantern.
الفانُوسُ يُضِيءُ! هُوَ أَجْمَلُ فانُوسٍ في الشّارِعِ.
The lantern lights up! It is the most beautiful lantern on the street.
تُغَنِّي هَنا مَعَ العائِلَةِ. لَيْلَةٌ سَعِيدَةٌ!
Hana sings with the family. What a happy night!
Why was Hana sad at the market?
Every lantern in the market was already sold.
Who had the idea to make a lantern at home?
Geddo said they could make one with their own hands.
What colors of paper did Hana cut?
She cut red and yellow paper for the lantern.
The Magic Kite. Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.
تَجِدُ هَنا طائِرَةً وَرَقِيَّةً قَدِيمَةً في البَيْتِ.
Hana finds an old kite in the house.
تَرْسُمُ هَنا عَلَيْها شَمْساً وَنُجُوماً صَفْراءَ.
Hana draws a sun and yellow stars on it.
في الحَدِيقَةِ، تَطِيرُ الطّائِرَةُ عالِياً.
In the garden, the kite flies high.
فَجْأَةً، تَلْمَعُ الطّائِرَةُ! إِنَّها طائِرَةٌ سِحْرِيَّةٌ!
Suddenly, the kite sparkles! It is a magic kite!
تَطِيرُ هَنا مَعَ الطّائِرَةِ فَوْقَ البُيُوتِ.
Hana flies with the kite over the houses.
تَرَى البَحْرَ الأَزْرَقَ وَالمَراكِبَ الصَّغِيرَةَ.
She sees the blue sea and the little boats.
تَرَى المَدْرَسَةَ وَالحَدِيقَةَ وَالشَّجَرَةَ الكَبِيرَةَ.
She sees the school, the garden, and the big tree.
تَنْزِلُ الطّائِرَةُ بِهُدُوءٍ في الحَدِيقَةِ.
The kite lands gently in the garden.
تَفْتَحُ هَنا عَيْنَيْها تَحْتَ الشَّجَرَةِ... هَلْ كانَ حُلْماً؟
Hana opens her eyes under the tree... was it a dream?
Where did Hana find the old kite?
She found the old kite in the house.
What did Hana see when she flew with the kite?
She saw the blue sea, little boats, her school, and the big tree.
How does the story end?
She opens her eyes under the tree, so maybe it was all a dream!
A Visit to Egypt. Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.
في الصَّيْفِ، سافَرَتْ هَنا إِلَى مِصْرَ.
In the summer, Hana traveled to Egypt.
اسْتَقْبَلَها جِدُّو وَتِيتَة في المَطارِ.
Geddo and Nena welcomed her at the airport.
ذَهَبَتِ العائِلَةُ إِلَى بَيْتِ جِدُّو في القاهِرَةِ.
The family went to Geddo's house in Cairo.
طَبَخَتْ تِيتَة طَعاماً مِصْرِيّاً لَذِيذاً.
Nena cooked delicious Egyptian food.
في اليَوْمِ الثّانِي، رَكِبَتْ هَنا جَمَلاً كَبِيراً.
On the second day, Hana rode a big camel.
رَأَتْ هَنا الأَهْراماتِ. ما أَكْبَرَها!
Hana saw the pyramids. How big they are!
فَجْأَةً، لَمْ تَجِدْ هَنا قُبَّعَتَها الحَمْراءَ!
Suddenly, Hana could not find her red hat!
أَخَذَتِ الرِّيحُ القُبَّعَةَ بَعِيداً في الرَّمْلِ.
The wind took the hat far away in the sand.
جَرَى الجَمَلُ وَأَمْسَكَ القُبَّعَةَ بِفَمِهِ!
The camel ran and caught the hat with its mouth!
ضَحِكَ الجَمِيعُ. الجَمَلُ صَدِيقٌ ذَكِيٌّ!
Everyone laughed. The camel is a clever friend!
في آخِرِ يَوْمٍ، رَكِبَتِ العائِلَةُ مَرْكَباً في نَهْرِ النِّيلِ.
On the last day, the family rode a boat on the Nile River.
قالَتْ هَنا: أُحِبُّ مِصْرَ! سَأَعُودُ كُلَّ صَيْفٍ.
Hana said: I love Egypt! I will come back every summer.
Who welcomed Hana at the airport?
Geddo and Nena were waiting for her at the airport.
What took Hana's red hat away?
The wind blew her red hat far away in the sand.
Who caught the hat?
The clever camel ran and caught the hat with its mouth.
The School Trip. Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.
يَوْمَ الخَمِيسِ، ذَهَبَتِ المَدْرَسَةُ في رِحْلَةٍ.
On Thursday, the school went on a trip.
حَمَلَتْ هَنا حَقِيبَتَها وَطَعامَها وَماءَها.
Hana carried her bag, her food, and her water.
وَصَلَتِ الحافِلَةُ إِلَى حَدِيقَةِ الحَيَواناتِ.
The bus arrived at the zoo.
رَأَتْ هَنا القُرُودَ تَلْعَبُ عَلَى الشَّجَرَةِ.
Hana saw the monkeys playing in the tree.
الفِيلُ الكَبِيرُ شَرِبَ الماءَ بِخُرْطُومِهِ.
The big elephant drank water with its trunk.
فَجْأَةً... أَيْنَ لَيْلَى؟ لَمْ تَجِدْها هَنا!
Suddenly... where is Layla? Hana could not find her!
بَحَثَتْ هَنا عِنْدَ القُرُودِ وَعِنْدَ الفِيلِ.
Hana looked by the monkeys and by the elephant.
أَخْبَرَتْ هَنا المُعَلِّمَةَ بِسُرْعَةٍ.
Hana quickly told the teacher.
وَجَدُوا لَيْلَى أَمامَ بَيْتِ البَطارِيقِ تَضْحَكُ.
They found Layla in front of the penguin house, laughing.
قالَتْ لَيْلَى: البَطارِيقُ تَمْشِي مِثْلِي!
Layla said: the penguins walk just like me!
أَكَلَ الأَصْدِقاءُ الطَّعامَ تَحْتَ شَجَرَةٍ كَبِيرَةٍ.
The friends ate their food under a big tree.
عادَتْ هَنا إِلَى البَيْتِ سَعِيدَةً وَمُتْعَبَةً.
Hana went back home happy and tired.
Where did the school bus go?
The bus took the school to the zoo.
Who got lost for a little while?
Layla disappeared for a little while and everyone looked for her.
Where did they find Layla?
She was watching the penguins and laughing at how they walk.
The Birthday Cake Surprise. Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.
غَداً عِيدُ مِيلادِ ماما.
Tomorrow is Mama's birthday.
قالَتْ هَنا لِبابا: نَصْنَعُ كَعْكَةً... سِرّاً!
Hana said to Baba: let's make a cake... secretly!
خَلَطَتْ هَنا الدَّقِيقَ وَالسُّكَّرَ وَالبَيْضَ.
Hana mixed the flour, the sugar, and the eggs.
أَضافَ بابا الشُّوكُولاتَةَ. رائِحَةٌ جَمِيلَةٌ!
Baba added the chocolate. What a lovely smell!
وَضَعا الكَعْكَةَ في الفُرْنِ وَانْتَظَرا.
They put the cake in the oven and waited.
يا إِلَهِي! نَسِيا الكَعْكَةَ! دُخانٌ أَسْوَدُ!
Oh no! They forgot the cake! Black smoke!
الكَعْكَةُ مُحْتَرِقَةٌ. جَلَسَتْ هَنا حَزِينَةً.
The cake is burnt. Hana sat down, sad.
حَتَّى القِطَّةُ هَرَبَتْ مِنَ الرّائِحَةِ!
Even the cat ran away from the smell!
قالَ بابا: عِنْدِي فِكْرَةٌ! نَصْنَعُ كَعْكَةً بِلا فُرْنٍ.
Baba said: I have an idea! We will make a cake without an oven.
وَضَعا بَسْكَوِيتاً وَكِرِيمَةً وَفَراوِلَةً حَمْراءَ.
They put biscuits, cream, and red strawberries.
في الصَّباحِ، رَأَتْ ماما الكَعْكَةَ وَفَرِحَتْ كَثِيراً.
In the morning, Mama saw the cake and was very happy.
قالَتْ ماما: أَجْمَلُ كَعْكَةٍ في العالَمِ! وَضَحِكَ الجَمِيعُ.
Mama said: the most beautiful cake in the world! And everyone laughed.
Whose birthday was it?
Hana and Baba made a secret cake for Mama's birthday.
What went wrong with the first cake?
They forgot the cake in the oven and it burnt with black smoke.
How did they fix the problem?
Baba had the idea to make a cake without an oven, using biscuits, cream, and strawberries.
The Camel Ride. Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.
هَنا تَذْهَبُ إِلى الصَّحْراءِ مَعَ بابا.
Hana goes to the desert with Baba.
تَرى هَنا جَمَلًا كَبيرًا وَجَميلًا.
Hana sees a big, beautiful camel.
الجَمَلُ عالٍ جِدًّا! هَنا خائِفَةٌ.
The camel is very tall! Hana is scared.
بابا يُساعِدُ هَنا. هِيَ تَرْكَبُ الجَمَلَ.
Baba helps Hana. She rides the camel.
الجَمَلُ يَمْشي. هَنا سَعيدَةٌ جِدًّا!
The camel walks. Hana is very happy!
الجَمَلُ يَأْكُلُ قُبَّعَةَ بابا! الجَميعُ يَضْحَكُ.
The camel eats Baba's hat! Everyone laughs.
Where does Hana go with Baba?
Hana goes to the desert with Baba, and that is where she sees the camel.
Why is Hana scared at first?
The camel is very tall, so Hana feels scared before she rides it.
What does the camel eat at the end?
The funny camel eats Baba's hat, and everyone laughs.
The Moon at Night. Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.
اللَّيْلُ جَميلٌ. هَنا تَنْظُرُ إِلى السَّماءِ.
The night is beautiful. Hana looks at the sky.
القَمَرُ كَبيرٌ وَجَميلٌ اللَّيْلَةَ.
The moon is big and beautiful tonight.
سَحابَةٌ كَبيرَةٌ تُغَطّي القَمَرَ. هَنا حَزينَةٌ.
A big cloud covers the moon. Hana is sad.
هَنا تَنْتَظِرُ مَعَ نينة في الشُّرْفَةِ.
Hana waits with Nena on the balcony.
السَّحابَةُ تَذْهَبُ! القَمَرُ يَعودُ.
The cloud goes away! The moon comes back.
هَنا تَنامُ وَالقَمَرُ يَنْظُرُ إِلَيْها.
Hana sleeps, and the moon watches over her.
What does Hana look at?
Hana looks at the beautiful night sky to see the moon.
What covers the moon?
A big cloud covers the moon, and Hana feels sad.
Who waits with Hana on the balcony?
Nena waits with Hana on the balcony until the cloud goes away.
The Little Goldfish. Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.
هَنا عِنْدَها سَمَكَةٌ صَغيرَةٌ وَجَميلَةٌ.
Hana has a small, beautiful fish.
كُلَّ يَوْمٍ تُطْعِمُ هَنا السَّمَكَةَ.
Every day Hana feeds the fish.
اليَوْمَ السَّمَكَةُ لا تَأْكُلُ. هَنا حَزينَةٌ.
Today the fish does not eat. Hana is sad.
ماما تَقولُ: «الماءُ لَيْسَ نَظيفًا».
Mama says the water is not clean.
هَنا تُنَظِّفُ الحَوْضَ بِماءٍ جَديدٍ.
Hana cleans the tank with fresh new water.
السَّمَكَةُ تَأْكُلُ وَتَرْقُصُ في الماءِ!
The fish eats and dances in the water!
What pet does Hana have?
Hana has a small, beautiful fish that she feeds every day.
Why does the fish stop eating?
Mama explains that the water is not clean, so the fish will not eat.
What does the fish do at the end?
After Hana cleans the tank, the happy fish eats and dances in the water.
The Butterfly in the Garden. Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.
هَنا تَلْعَبُ في الحَديقَةِ الجَميلَةِ.
Hana plays in the beautiful garden.
تَرى فَراشَةً صَغيرَةً عَلى الوَرْدَةِ.
She sees a small butterfly on the rose.
هَنا تَجْري خَلْفَ الفَراشَةِ.
Hana runs after the butterfly.
الفَراشَةُ تَطيرُ بَعيدًا. هَنا حَزينَةٌ.
The butterfly flies far away. Hana is sad.
هَنا تَجْلِسُ بِهُدوءٍ وَلا تَتَحَرَّكُ.
Hana sits quietly and does not move.
الفَراشَةُ تَقِفُ عَلى يَدِها! يا لَلْجَمالِ!
The butterfly lands on her hand! How beautiful!
Where does Hana play?
Hana plays in the beautiful garden, where she sees the butterfly.
What does Hana see on the rose?
A small butterfly is sitting on the rose in the garden.
How does Hana get the butterfly to come to her?
When Hana sits quietly without moving, the butterfly lands on her hand.
The Lost Tooth. Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.
سِنُّ هَنا يَتَحَرَّكُ!
Hana's tooth is wiggling!
هَنا تَأْكُلُ تُفّاحَةً كَبيرَةً.
Hana eats a big apple.
السِّنُّ يَقَعُ! لكِنْ أَيْنَ هُوَ؟
The tooth falls out! But where is it?
هَنا تَبْحَثُ عَنِ السِّنِّ في كُلِّ مَكانٍ.
Hana looks for the tooth everywhere.
السِّنُّ في التُّفّاحَةِ! الجَميعُ يَضْحَكُ.
The tooth is in the apple! Everyone laughs.
ماما تَقولُ: «الآنَ أَنْتِ كَبيرَةٌ يا هَنا!».
Mama says that Hana is a big girl now!
What is wiggling at the start of the story?
Hana's tooth is wiggling, ready to fall out.
Where does Hana find her tooth?
The tooth fell out into the big apple she was eating!
What does Mama say at the end?
Mama tells Hana that losing a tooth means she is a big girl now.
The Ice-Cream Seller. Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.
اليَوْمُ حارٌّ جِدًّا. الشَّمْسُ كَبيرَةٌ.
Today is very hot. The sun is big.
هَنا تَسْمَعُ جَرَسَ بائِعِ المُثَلَّجاتِ.
Hana hears the ice-cream seller's bell.
هَنا تَجْري إِلى الشّارِعِ بِسُرْعَةٍ.
Hana runs quickly to the street.
لا نُقودَ مَعَ هَنا! هِيَ حَزينَةٌ.
Hana has no money! She is sad.
البائِعُ يَبْتَسِمُ: «ساعِديني وَخُذي واحِدَةً».
The seller smiles and says she can help him and take one.
هَنا تُساعِدُ البائِعَ وَتَأْكُلُ مُثَلَّجاتِ الفَراوِلَةِ!
Hana helps the seller and eats a strawberry ice cream!
What is the weather like in this story?
It is a very hot day, perfect for ice cream.
Why is Hana sad at first?
Hana runs to the street but she has no money for ice cream.
What flavour of ice cream does Hana eat?
After helping the kind seller, Hana enjoys a strawberry ice cream.
The Little Lamb. Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.
في بَيْتِ جَدّو خَروفٌ صَغيرٌ وَأَبْيَضُ.
At Geddo's house there is a small white lamb.
هَنا تُحِبُّ الخَروفَ الصَّغيرَ كَثيرًا.
Hana loves the little lamb very much.
البابُ مَفْتوحٌ! الخَروفُ لَيْسَ هُنا!
The door is open! The lamb is not here!
هَنا وَجَدّو يَبْحَثانِ في الحَديقَةِ.
Hana and Geddo search in the garden.
الخَروفُ في المَطْبَخِ يَأْكُلُ الخَسَّ!
The lamb is in the kitchen, eating the lettuce!
نينة تَضْحَكُ: «هذا خَروفٌ جَوْعانُ!».
Nena laughs and says that is one hungry lamb!
What colour is the little lamb?
Geddo's little lamb is small and white.
Where do they find the lamb?
The lamb sneaked into the kitchen through the open door.
What is the lamb eating?
The hungry lamb is eating the lettuce in the kitchen.
The School Library Book. Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.
هَنا تَذْهَبُ إِلى مَكْتَبَةِ المَدْرَسَةِ.
Hana goes to the school library.
تَجِدُ كِتابًا جَميلًا عَنِ القِطَطِ.
She finds a beautiful book about cats.
تَأْخُذُ هَنا الكِتابَ إِلى البَيْتِ.
Hana takes the book home.
بَعْدَ أُسْبوعٍ، لا تَجِدُ الكِتابَ!
After a week, she cannot find the book!
هَنا تَبْحَثُ... الكِتابُ تَحْتَ السَّريرِ!
Hana searches everywhere... the book is under the bed!
تُعيدُ هَنا الكِتابَ، وَالمُعَلِّمَةُ سَعيدَةٌ.
Hana returns the book, and the teacher is happy.
What is the library book about?
Hana finds a beautiful book about cats in the school library.
Where does Hana find the lost book?
After searching everywhere, Hana finds the book under her bed.
How does the teacher feel when Hana returns the book?
The teacher is happy because Hana brought the book back.
The Sandcastle Competition. Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.
اليَوْمَ مُسابَقَةٌ كَبيرَةٌ عَلى الشّاطِئِ.
Today there is a big competition at the beach.
كُلُّ طِفْلٍ يَبْني قَصْرًا مِنَ الرَّمْلِ.
Every child builds a castle out of sand.
هَنا وَلَيْلى تَعْمَلانِ مَعًا.
Hana and Layla work together.
هَنا تَمْلَأُ الدَّلْوَ، وَلَيْلى تَبْني.
Hana fills the bucket, and Layla builds.
مَوْجَةٌ كَبيرَةٌ تَأْتي! يا لَلْمُصيبَةِ!
A big wave comes! Oh no!
نِصْفُ القَصْرِ يَخْتَفي في الماءِ.
Half of the castle disappears into the water.
لكِنَّ البِنْتَيْنِ لا تَسْتَسْلِمانِ. تَبْنيانِ بِسُرْعَةٍ.
But the two girls do not give up. They build quickly.
تَضَعانِ أَصْدافًا جَميلَةً عَلى القَصْرِ.
They put beautiful shells on the castle.
القَصْرُ يَفوزُ بِالجائِزَةِ الأولى!
The castle wins first prize!
Where is the competition?
The big sandcastle competition is at the beach.
What ruins half of the castle?
A big wave comes and washes away half of the castle.
What do the girls put on the castle?
They decorate the castle with beautiful shells, and it wins first prize.
Mama's Famous Dish. Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.
اليَوْمَ تَطْبُخُ ماما الكُشَري المِصْرِيَّ.
Today Mama is cooking Egypt's famous rice and lentil dish.
هَنا تَقولُ: «أَنا أُساعِدُكِ يا ماما!».
Hana says she will help Mama!
هَنا تَغْسِلُ الأَرُزَّ بِالماءِ.
Hana washes the rice with water.
ماما تَقْلي البَصَلَ. رائِحَتُهُ جَميلَةٌ!
Mama fries the onions. They smell wonderful!
المَعْكَرونَةُ وَالعَدَسُ في القِدْرِ الكَبيرِ.
The pasta and the lentils go into the big pot.
هَنا تُمْسِكُ السُّكَّرَ! «قِفي! هذا لَيْسَ مِلْحًا!».
Hana grabs the sugar! Mama shouts to stop, that is not salt!
ماما تَضْحَكُ وَتُعْطيها المِلْحَ الصَّحيحَ.
Mama laughs and gives her the right salt.
هَنا تَسْكُبُ الصَّلْصَةَ الحَمْراءَ فَوْقَ الطَّبَقِ.
Hana pours the red sauce over the dish.
بابا يَأْكُلُ وَيَقولُ: «أَلَذُّ طَبَقٍ في مِصْرَ!».
Baba eats and says it is the tastiest dish in Egypt!
What does Hana wash for the dish?
Hana helps Mama by washing the rice with water.
What does Hana almost put in the pot by mistake?
Hana grabs the sugar by mistake, but Mama stops her just in time.
What does Baba say about the dish?
Baba loves it and says it is the tastiest dish in Egypt.
The First Snow. Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.
هَنا تُسافِرُ مَعَ العائِلَةِ في الشِّتاءِ.
Hana travels with her family in the winter.
الجَوُّ بارِدٌ جِدًّا هُنا!
The weather is very cold here!
في الصَّباحِ، تَنْظُرُ هَنا مِنَ النّافِذَةِ.
In the morning, Hana looks out of the window.
كُلُّ شَيْءٍ أَبْيَضُ! هذا هُوَ الثَّلْجُ!
Everything is white! This is snow!
تَلْبَسُ هَنا مِعْطَفًا وَقُفّازَيْنِ.
Hana puts on a coat and gloves.
هَنا وَبابا يَبْنيانِ رَجُلَ ثَلْجٍ.
Hana and Baba build a snowman.
لا جَزَرَ لِلْأَنْفِ! هَنا حَزينَةٌ.
There is no carrot for the nose! Hana is sad.
بابا يَضَعُ مَوْزَةً مَكانَ الأَنْفِ!
Baba puts a banana where the nose should be!
رَجُلُ الثَّلْجِ مُضْحِكٌ! الجَميعُ يَضْحَكُ وَيُصَوِّرُ.
The snowman looks so funny! Everyone laughs and takes photos.
What does Hana see from the window in the morning?
Hana looks out and sees everything covered in white snow for the first time.
What do Hana and Baba build?
Hana and Baba build a snowman together in the snow.
What do they use for the snowman's nose?
There is no carrot, so Baba uses a banana, and it looks very funny.
Eid Morning. Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.
غَدًا العيدُ! هَنا سَعيدَةٌ جِدًّا.
Tomorrow is Eid! Hana is very happy.
ماما تَشْتَري لِهَنا فُسْتانًا جَديدًا.
Mama buys Hana a new dress.
في الصَّباحِ، تَسْتَيْقِظُ هَنا مُبَكِّرًا.
In the morning, Hana wakes up early.
تَذْهَبُ العائِلَةُ إِلى صَلاةِ العيدِ.
The family goes to the Eid prayer.
بَعْدَ الصَّلاةِ، حَلْوى وَكَعْكٌ لَذيذٌ.
After the prayer, there are sweets and delicious cookies.
لكِنْ أَيْنَ جَدّو؟ هَنا لا تَراهُ.
But where is Geddo? Hana cannot see him.
الجَرَسُ يَرِنُّ. هَنا تَفْتَحُ البابَ.
The doorbell rings. Hana opens the door.
جَدّو هُنا! وَمَعَهُ هَدِيَّةٌ كَبيرَةٌ.
Geddo is here! And he has a big present.
العيدِيَّةُ وَحُضْنٌ كَبيرٌ! أَجْمَلُ عيدٍ!
Eid money and a big hug! The best Eid ever!
What does Mama buy for Hana?
Mama buys Hana a beautiful new dress to wear on Eid.
Who can Hana not find in the morning?
Hana looks around and cannot see Geddo anywhere.
What does Geddo bring when he arrives?
Geddo arrives at the door with a big present, Eid money, and a big hug.
The Talking Parrot. Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.
في بَيْتِ نينة بَبَّغاءٌ أَخْضَرُ.
At Nena's house there is a green parrot.
البَبَّغاءُ يَتَكَلَّمُ! يَقولُ: «صَباحُ الخَيْرِ!».
The parrot talks! It says good morning!
هَنا تُعَلِّمُ البَبَّغاءَ كَلِماتٍ جَديدَةً.
Hana teaches the parrot new words.
نينة تَبْحَثُ عَنِ البَسْكَويتِ: «أَيْنَ البَسْكَويتُ؟».
Nena is looking for the biscuits and asks where they are.
البَبَّغاءُ يَصيحُ: «القِطُّ أَخَذَها! القِطُّ أَخَذَها!».
The parrot shouts that the cat took them!
لكِنَّ القِطَّ نائِمٌ عَلى الكُرْسِيِّ.
But the cat is fast asleep on the chair.
هَنا تَنْظُرُ إِلى قَفَصِ البَبَّغاءِ.
Hana looks at the parrot's cage.
البَسْكَويتُ داخِلَ القَفَصِ! البَبَّغاءُ هُوَ السّارِقُ!
The biscuits are inside the cage! The parrot is the thief!
البَبَّغاءُ يَقولُ: «أَنا آسِفٌ! أَنا آسِفٌ!».
The parrot says it is sorry, it is sorry!
What colour is the parrot?
Nena has a green parrot that can talk.
Who does the parrot say took the biscuits?
The parrot blames the cat, but the cat is asleep on the chair.
Who really took the biscuits?
Hana finds the biscuits inside the cage, so the parrot is the real thief!
The Bicycle Lesson. Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.
هَنا عِنْدَها دَرّاجَةٌ جَديدَةٌ وَحَمْراءُ.
Hana has a new red bicycle.
لكِنَّها لا تَعْرِفُ الرُّكوبَ بَعْدُ.
But she does not know how to ride it yet.
بابا يَقولُ: «أَنا أُمْسِكُ الدَّرّاجَةَ، لا تَخافي».
Baba says he will hold the bicycle, so do not be afraid.
هَنا تَرْكَبُ، وَبابا يَجْري خَلْفَها.
Hana rides, and Baba runs behind her.
هَنا تَقَعُ عَلى العُشْبِ. آه!
Hana falls onto the grass. Ouch!
هَنا حَزينَةٌ وَتُريدُ أَنْ تَتَوَقَّفَ.
Hana is sad and wants to stop.
بابا يَقولُ: «مَرَّةً أَخيرَةً، أَنْتِ قَوِيَّةٌ!».
Baba says one more try, because she is strong!
هَنا تَرْكَبُ وَحْدَها! بابا لا يُمْسِكُ!
Hana rides all by herself! Baba is not holding on!
«أَنا أَرْكَبُ! أَنا أَرْكَبُ!» تَصيحُ هَنا بِفَرَحٍ.
I am riding, I am riding, shouts Hana with joy!
What colour is Hana's new bicycle?
Hana has a brand new red bicycle.
Where does Hana fall?
Hana falls onto the soft grass while learning to ride.
What does Baba say after Hana falls?
Baba encourages Hana to try one more time because she is strong, and she rides by herself!
The Swimming Lesson. Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.
اليَوْمَ أَوَّلُ دَرْسِ سِباحَةٍ لِهَنا.
Today is Hana's first swimming lesson.
المَسْبَحُ كَبيرٌ، وَهَنا خائِفَةٌ قَليلًا.
The pool is big, and Hana is a little scared.
المُدَرِّبَةُ تَقولُ: «الماءُ صَديقُكِ يا هَنا».
The coach says the water is her friend.
هَنا تَجْلِسُ وَتُحَرِّكُ رِجْلَيْها في الماءِ.
Hana sits and kicks her legs in the water.
ثُمَّ تَنْزِلُ إِلى الماءِ بِبُطْءٍ.
Then she goes into the water slowly.
الماءُ في أَنْفِها! هَنا لا تُحِبُّ هذا.
Water goes up her nose! Hana does not like that.
المُدَرِّبَةُ تُعَلِّمُها أَنْ تَنْفُخَ فُقاعاتٍ.
The coach teaches her to blow bubbles.
بَعْدَ قَليلٍ، هَنا تَعومُ مِثْلَ سَمَكَةٍ صَغيرَةٍ!
Soon, Hana is floating like a little fish!
ماما تُصَفِّقُ: «أَنْتِ سَمَكَتي الصَّغيرَةُ!».
Mama claps and calls her my little fish!
How does Hana feel at her first swimming lesson?
The pool is big, so Hana feels a little scared at first.
What goes up Hana's nose?
Water goes up her nose, and she does not like that at all.
What does the coach teach Hana to do?
The coach teaches her to blow bubbles, and soon she floats like a little fish.
Geddo's Old Radio. Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.
عِنْدَ جَدّو راديو قَديمٌ وَبُنِّيٌّ.
Geddo has an old brown radio.
كُلَّ مَساءٍ يَسْمَعُ جَدّو الأَغاني القَديمَةَ.
Every evening Geddo listens to the old songs.
اليَوْمَ الرّاديو لا يَعْمَلُ!
Today the radio does not work!
جَدّو حَزينٌ جِدًّا. هَنا تُريدُ أَنْ تُساعِدَ.
Geddo is very sad. Hana wants to help.
هَنا تَنْظُرُ خَلْفَ الرّاديو الكَبيرِ.
Hana looks behind the big radio.
السِّلْكُ لَيْسَ في الكَهْرَباءِ!
The wire is not plugged in!
القِطُّ لَعِبَ بِالسِّلْكِ في اللَّيْلِ!
The cat played with the wire in the night!
هَنا تُعيدُ السِّلْكَ، وَالأَغاني تَعودُ.
Hana plugs the wire back in, and the songs come back.
جَدّو وَهَنا يَرْقُصانِ عَلى الأَغاني القَديمَةِ!
Geddo and Hana dance to the old songs!
What does Geddo listen to every evening?
Geddo loves listening to the old songs on his radio every evening.
Why did the radio stop working?
Hana looks behind the radio and finds the wire is not plugged in.
Who played with the wire?
The cat played with the wire in the night and pulled it out.
A Picnic by the Nile. Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.
في يَوْمِ الجُمُعَةِ، ذَهَبَتِ العائِلَةُ إِلى النّيلِ.
On Friday, the family went to the Nile.
حَمَلَتْ ماما سَلَّةً كَبيرَةً مِنَ الطَّعامِ.
Mama carried a big basket of food.
جَلَسوا تَحْتَ شَجَرَةٍ كَبيرَةٍ بِجانِبِ الماءِ.
They sat under a big tree beside the water.
شاهَدَتْ هَنا المَراكِبَ الجَميلَةَ عَلى النّيلِ.
Hana watched the beautiful boats on the Nile.
فَتَحَتْ ماما السَّلَّةَ: سَنْدَويشاتٌ وَفاكِهَةٌ وَعَصيرٌ.
Mama opened the basket: sandwiches, fruit, and juice.
جاءَتْ بَطَّةٌ صَغيرَةٌ إِلى هَنا.
A little duck came up to Hana.
أَعْطَتْ هَنا البَطَّةَ قِطْعَةَ خُبْزٍ.
Hana gave the duck a piece of bread.
فَجْأَةً، جاءَتْ عَشْرُ بَطّاتٍ جَوْعانَةٍ!
Suddenly, ten hungry ducks came!
البَطّاتُ جَرَتْ خَلْفَ هَنا وَالسَّنْدَويشاتِ!
The ducks chased Hana and the sandwiches!
ضَحِكَ بابا كَثيرًا وَساعَدَ هَنا.
Baba laughed a lot and helped Hana.
أَكَلَتِ العائِلَةُ وَشاهَدَتْ غُروبَ الشَّمْسِ.
The family ate and watched the sunset.
قالَتْ هَنا: «أَجْمَلُ يَوْمٍ بِجانِبِ النّيلِ!».
Hana said it was the most beautiful day by the Nile!
The family went for a picnic by the Nile and sat under a big tree.
What did Hana give the little duck?
Hana kindly gave the little duck a piece of bread.
What happened after Hana fed the first duck?
Ten hungry ducks suddenly came and chased Hana and the sandwiches!
The Art Competition. Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.
أَعْلَنَتِ المُعَلِّمَةُ عَنْ مُسابَقَةِ رَسْمٍ كَبيرَةٍ.
The teacher announced a big art competition.
فَكَّرَتْ هَنا: «ماذا أَرْسُمُ؟».
Hana thought about what she should draw.
رَسَمَتْ لَيْلى قِطَّةً جَميلَةً.
Layla drew a beautiful cat.
قَرَّرَتْ هَنا أَنْ تَرْسُمَ البَحْرَ.
Hana decided to draw the sea.
رَسَمَتْ هَنا كُلَّ يَوْمٍ بَعْدَ المَدْرَسَةِ.
Hana painted every day after school.
قَبْلَ المُسابَقَةِ، وَقَعَ العَصيرُ عَلى الرَّسْمِ!
Before the competition, juice spilled on the painting!
بَكَتْ هَنا. في الرَّسْمِ بُقْعَةٌ بُرْتُقالِيَّةٌ.
Hana cried. There was an orange stain on the painting.
ثُمَّ نَظَرَتْ هَنا إِلى البُقْعَةِ وَفَكَّرَتْ.
Then Hana looked at the stain and thought.
حَوَّلَتْ هَنا البُقْعَةَ إِلى شَمْسٍ جَميلَةٍ!
Hana turned the stain into a beautiful setting sun!
الآنَ في الرَّسْمِ بَحْرٌ وَغُروبٌ ذَهَبِيٌّ.
Now the painting had a sea and a golden sunset.
فازَتْ هَنا بِالمَرْكَزِ الأَوَّلِ!
Hana won first place!
قالَتِ المُعَلِّمَةُ: «أَجْمَلُ غُروبٍ رَأَيْتُهُ!».
The teacher said it was the most beautiful sunset she had ever seen!
What did Hana decide to draw?
Hana decided to draw the sea, while Layla drew a cat.
What spilled on Hana's painting?
Juice spilled on the painting and left an orange stain.
How did Hana fix the stain?
Hana was clever: she turned the orange stain into a golden sunset and won first place!
A Visit to the Pyramids. Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.
رَكِبَتِ العائِلَةُ السَّيّارَةَ إِلى الجيزَةِ.
The family drove in the car to Giza.
مِنْ بَعيدٍ، رَأَتْ هَنا الأَهْراماتِ الكَبيرَةَ.
From far away, Hana saw the great pyramids.
قالَ بابا: «بَنى المِصْرِيّونَ هذا مِنْ زَمَنٍ بَعيدٍ».
Baba said the Egyptians built this long, long ago.
وَقَفَتْ هَنا كَأَنَّها تَحْمِلُ الهَرَمَ!
Hana stood as if she was holding up the pyramid!
رَكِبَتْ هَنا حِصانًا صَغيرًا مَعَ بابا.
Hana rode a small horse with Baba.
رَأَوْا أَبا الهَوْلِ بِوَجْهِ الإِنْسانِ.
They saw the Sphinx with its human face.
سَأَلَتْ هَنا: «أَيْنَ أَنْفُ أَبي الهَوْلِ؟».
Hana asked where the Sphinx's nose was.
ضَحِكَ جَدّو: «هذا سِرٌّ قَديمٌ جِدًّا!».
Geddo laughed and said that is a very old secret!
كانَ الجَوُّ حارًّا، فَشَرِبوا عَصيرَ قَصَبٍ.
The weather was hot, so they drank sugar-cane juice.
في المَتْحَفِ، رَأَتْ هَنا ذَهَبَ المُلوكِ.
In the museum, Hana saw the gold of the kings.
اِشْتَرَتْ هَنا كِتابًا صَغيرًا عَنِ الفَراعِنَةِ.
Hana bought a little book about the pharaohs.
قالَتْ هَنا: «أَنا فَخورَةٌ لِأَنَّني مِصْرِيَّةٌ!».
Hana said she was proud to be Egyptian!
What did Hana see from far away?
On the drive to Giza, Hana saw the great pyramids from far away.
What is missing from the Sphinx?
Hana noticed the Sphinx has no nose, and Geddo said it is a very old secret.
What did the family drink because it was hot?
The weather was hot, so they drank cool sugar-cane juice.
The Planetarium Trip. Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.
ذَهَبَ الصَّفُّ في رِحْلَةٍ إِلى القُبَّةِ الفَلَكِيَّةِ.
The class went on a trip to the planetarium.
قالَتْ لَيْلى: «ما هِيَ القُبَّةُ الفَلَكِيَّةُ؟».
Layla asked what a planetarium is.
دَخَلوا قاعَةً كَبيرَةً وَمُظْلِمَةً.
They entered a big, dark hall.
خافَتْ لَيْلى مِنَ الظَّلامِ.
Layla was scared of the dark.
أَمْسَكَتْ هَنا بِيَدِ صَديقَتِها.
Hana held her friend's hand.
فَجْأَةً، ظَهَرَتْ آلافُ النُّجومِ فَوْقَهُم!
Suddenly, thousands of stars appeared above them!
قالَتْ لَيْلى: «هذا أَجْمَلُ شَيْءٍ رَأَيْتُهُ!».
Layla said it was the most beautiful thing she had ever seen!
رَأَوْا كَواكِبَ كَبيرَةً وَقَمَرًا قَريبًا.
They saw big planets and a moon that looked very close.
قالَتِ المُعَلِّمَةُ: «الشَّمْسُ نَجْمٌ كَبيرٌ».
The teacher said the sun is a big star.
رَأَوْا نَجْمًا يَجْري في السَّماءِ!
They saw a shooting star race across the sky!
أَغْمَضَتْ هَنا عَيْنَيْها وَتَمَنَّتْ أُمْنِيَةً.
Hana closed her eyes and made a wish.
في اللَّيْلِ، نَظَرَتْ هَنا إِلى النُّجومِ مِنَ النّافِذَةِ.
That night, Hana watched the stars from her window.
Where did the class go on their trip?
The class went on a school trip to the planetarium to see the stars.
Who was scared of the dark hall?
Layla was scared of the dark, so Hana held her hand.
What did the teacher say about the sun?
The teacher explained that the sun is really a big star.
Prophet Adam (peace be upon him). Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.
اللهُ خَلَقَ آدَمَ عَلَيْهِ السَّلام.
Allah created Adam (peace be upon him).
خَلَقَهُ اللهُ مِنَ التُّرابِ.
Allah created him from the dust of the earth.
آدَمُ هُوَ أَوَّلُ إِنْسانٍ. أَكْرَمَهُ اللهُ.
Adam was the first human. Allah honoured him.
عَلَّمَهُ اللهُ أَسْماءَ كُلِّ الأَشْياءِ.
Allah taught him the names of all things.
أَمَرَ اللهُ المَلائِكَةَ أَنْ تُكْرِمَ آدَمَ.
Allah told the angels to honour Adam.
سَكَنَ آدَمُ في الجَنَّةِ الجَميلَةِ.
Adam lived in the beautiful garden.
ثُمَّ نَزَلَ آدَمُ إِلى الأَرْضِ.
Then Adam came down to the earth.
تابَ آدَمُ، وَاللهُ غَفورٌ رَحيمٌ.
Adam turned back to Allah, and Allah is most forgiving and kind.
Who was the first human?
Adam (peace be upon him) was the very first human, and Allah honoured him.
From what did Allah create Adam?
Allah created Adam from the dust of the earth, which shows Allah's great power.
What did Adam do after his mistake?
Adam turned back to Allah and asked forgiveness. Allah is always ready to forgive us when we are sorry.
Prophet Nuh (peace be upon him) and the Ark. Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.
نوحٌ عَلَيْهِ السَّلام نَبِيٌّ كَريمٌ.
Nuh (peace be upon him) was a noble prophet.
قالَ لِقَوْمِهِ: اعْبُدوا اللهَ وَحْدَهُ.
He told his people to worship only Allah.
لكِنَّ قَوْمَهُ لَمْ يَسْمَعوا لَهُ.
But his people did not listen to him.
دَعاهُمْ نوحٌ سَنَواتٍ كَثيرَةً بِصَبْرٍ.
Nuh called them for many years, with patience.
أَمَرَهُ اللهُ أَنْ يَصْنَعَ سَفينَةً كَبيرَةً.
Allah told him to build a big ship.
حَمَلَ فيها المُؤْمِنينَ وَأَزْواجًا مِنَ الحَيَوانِ.
He carried the believers and pairs of animals in it.
نَزَلَ المَطَرُ الشَّديدُ، وَجاءَ الطّوفانُ.
Heavy rain fell, and the great flood came.
غَطّى الماءُ الأَرْضَ كُلَّها.
The water covered all the land.
أَنْقَذَ اللهُ نوحًا وَالمُؤْمِنينَ في السَّفينَةِ.
Allah saved Nuh and the believers in the ship.
تَوَقَّفَ المَطَرُ، وَنَزَلوا بِسَلامٍ.
The rain stopped, and they came out safely.
الصَّبْرُ وَالإيمانُ بِاللهِ يُنْقِذانِنا.
Patience and faith in Allah keep us safe.
What did Nuh tell his people to do?
Nuh told his people to worship only Allah, but most of them did not listen.
What did Allah tell Nuh to build?
Allah told Nuh to build a big ship to save the believers from the flood.
How was Nuh saved?
Allah saved Nuh and the believers in the ship. Faith and patience keep us safe.
Prophet Ibrahim (peace be upon him) and the Fire. Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.
إِبْراهيمُ عَلَيْهِ السَّلام نَبِيٌّ عَظيمٌ.
Ibrahim (peace be upon him) was a great prophet.
كانَ قَوْمُهُ يَعْبُدونَ أَصْنامًا مِنَ الحَجَرِ.
His people used to worship idols made of stone.
قالَ إِبْراهيمُ: هذِهِ لا تَسْمَعُ وَلا تَنْفَعُ.
Ibrahim said these idols cannot hear or help.
دَعاهُمْ إِلى عِبادَةِ اللهِ وَحْدَهُ.
He called them to worship Allah alone.
دَخَلَ إِبْراهيمُ وَكَسَرَ الأَصْنامَ.
Ibrahim went in and broke the idols.
غَضِبَ قَوْمُهُ غَضَبًا شَديدًا.
His people became very angry.
أَشْعَلوا نارًا كَبيرَةً لِيُؤْذوهُ.
They lit a huge fire to harm him.
لكِنَّ إِبْراهيمَ وَثِقَ بِاللهِ.
But Ibrahim trusted in Allah.
أَمَرَ اللهُ النّارَ أَنْ تَكونَ بَرْدًا وَسَلامًا.
Allah made the fire cool and safe.
خَرَجَ إِبْراهيمُ سالِمًا لَمْ يُصِبْهُ أَذًى.
Ibrahim came out safe, with no harm at all.
مَنْ وَثِقَ بِاللهِ حَفِظَهُ اللهُ.
Whoever trusts in Allah, Allah protects them.
What did Ibrahim's people worship?
Ibrahim's people worshipped idols of stone, but he taught them to worship only Allah.
What did Ibrahim do to the idols?
Ibrahim broke the idols to show they were only stone and could not help anyone.
What happened to the fire?
Allah made the fire cool and safe. When we trust Allah, Allah protects us.
Building the Kaaba and the Zamzam Water. Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.
إِبْراهيمُ وَابْنُهُ إِسْماعيلُ عَلَيْهِمَا السَّلام.
Ibrahim and his son Ismail (peace be upon them).
تَرَكَ إِبْراهيمُ زَوْجَتَهُ وَطِفْلَهُ في وادٍ.
Ibrahim left his wife and baby in a dry valley.
لَمْ يَكُنْ هُناكَ ماءٌ في الصَّحْراءِ.
There was no water in the desert.
سَعَتِ الأُمُّ تَبْحَثُ عَنِ الماءِ.
The mother ran, looking for water.
أَخْرَجَ اللهُ ماءً قُرْبَ الطِّفْلِ.
Allah brought out water near the baby.
هذا هُوَ ماءُ زَمْزَم المُبارَكُ.
This is the blessed water of Zamzam.
كَبِرَ إِسْماعيلُ، وَبَنَيا بَيْتًا لِلهِ.
Ismail grew up, and they built a house for Allah.
بَنَيا الكَعْبَةَ مَعًا بِأَيْديهِما.
Together they built the Kaaba with their hands.
دَعَوَا اللهَ أَنْ يَتَقَبَّلَ عَمَلَهُما.
They asked Allah to accept their work.
اليَوْمَ يَتَوَجَّهُ المُسْلِمونَ إِلى الكَعْبَةِ.
Today Muslims face the Kaaba when they pray.
الطّاعَةُ وَالثِّقَةُ بِاللهِ فيها الخَيْرُ.
Obeying and trusting Allah always brings good.
What water did Allah bring out in the desert?
Allah brought out the blessed Zamzam water near baby Ismail. It still flows today.
What did Ibrahim and Ismail build together?
Ibrahim and his son Ismail built the Kaaba, the house for worshipping Allah.
What do Muslims do towards the Kaaba?
Muslims all around the world face the Kaaba when they pray, uniting them together.
Prophet Yusuf (peace be upon him). Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.
يوسُفُ عَلَيْهِ السَّلام صَبِيٌّ طَيِّبٌ.
Yusuf (peace be upon him) was a good young boy.
رَأى في المَنامِ نُجومًا تَسْجُدُ لَهُ.
He saw in a dream stars bowing down to him.
أَحَبَّهُ أَبوهُ يَعْقوبُ عَلَيْهِ السَّلام كَثيرًا.
His father Yaqub (peace be upon him) loved him very much.
غارَ إِخْوَتُهُ مِنْهُ وَدَبَّروا خُطَّةً.
His brothers were jealous and made a plan.
أَلْقَوْهُ في بِئْرٍ عَميقَةٍ وَتَرَكوهُ.
They put him in a deep well and left him.
وَجَدَتْهُ قافِلَةٌ وَأَخَذَتْهُ إِلى مِصْرَ.
A caravan found him and took him to Egypt.
عاشَ يوسُفُ في بَيْتٍ كَبيرٍ وَصَبَرَ.
Yusuf lived in a big house and stayed patient.
ظُلِمَ يوسُفُ وَدَخَلَ السِّجْنَ بِلا ذَنْبٍ.
Yusuf was wronged and put in prison for nothing.
أَعْطاهُ اللهُ عِلْمَ تَفْسيرِ الأَحْلامِ.
Allah gave him the knowledge to explain dreams.
فَسَّرَ حُلْمَ المَلِكِ عَنِ السَّنَواتِ وَالطَّعامِ.
He explained the king's dream about the years and food.
جَعَلَهُ المَلِكُ وَزيرًا عَلى خَزائِنِ مِصْرَ.
The king made him a minister over Egypt's stores.
جاءَ إِخْوَتُهُ، فَعَفا عَنْهُمْ وَسامَحَهُمْ.
His brothers came, and he forgave them kindly.
جَمَعَ اللهُ العائِلَةَ مَرَّةً أُخْرى بِفَرَحٍ.
Allah brought the family back together with joy.
الصَّبْرُ وَالعَفْوُ يَرْفَعانِ صاحِبَهُما.
Patience and forgiveness lift a person up.
What did Yusuf's brothers do to him?
His brothers were jealous and put him in a well, but Allah had a good plan for Yusuf.
What special gift did Allah give Yusuf?
Allah gave Yusuf the knowledge to explain dreams, which helped him and all of Egypt.
What did Yusuf do when his brothers came back?
Yusuf forgave his brothers with a kind heart. Forgiveness is a beautiful quality Allah loves.
Prophet Musa (peace be upon him) — The Baby in the River. Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.
كانَ في مِصْرَ مَلِكٌ ظالِمٌ اسْمُهُ فِرْعَوْنُ.
In Egypt there was a cruel king called Pharaoh.
كانَ فِرْعَوْنُ يُؤْذي بَني إِسْرائيلَ.
Pharaoh treated the Children of Israel badly.
وُلِدَ موسى عَلَيْهِ السَّلام في ذلِكَ الوَقْتِ.
Musa (peace be upon him) was born at that time.
خافَتْ أُمُّهُ عَلَيْهِ خَوْفًا شَديدًا.
His mother was very afraid for him.
أَلْهَمَها اللهُ أَنْ تَضَعَهُ في صُنْدوقٍ.
Allah inspired her to place him in a basket.
وَضَعَتِ الصُّنْدوقَ في النَّهْرِ بِثِقَةٍ.
She put the basket in the river, trusting Allah.
وَصَلَ الصُّنْدوقُ إِلى قَصْرِ فِرْعَوْنَ.
The basket reached Pharaoh's palace.
أَحَبَّتْهُ زَوْجَةُ فِرْعَوْنَ وَأَخَذَتْهُ.
Pharaoh's wife loved him and took him in.
أَرْجَعَ اللهُ موسى إِلى أُمِّهِ لِتُرْضِعَهُ.
Allah returned Musa to his mother to care for him.
كَبِرَ موسى في القَصْرِ بِحِفْظِ اللهِ.
Musa grew up in the palace under Allah's care.
اللهُ يَحْفَظُ مَنْ يَتَوَكَّلُ عَلَيْهِ.
Allah protects those who rely on Him.
Who was the cruel king of Egypt?
Pharaoh was the cruel king, but Allah protected baby Musa from all his harm.
Where did Musa's mother place him?
Allah inspired his mother to place him in a basket in the river, trusting Allah to keep him safe.
What did Allah do for Musa's mother?
Allah returned Musa to his own mother to care for him. Allah's plan is always the best plan.
Prophet Musa (peace be upon him) — Parting the Sea. Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.
جَعَلَ اللهُ موسى نَبِيًّا كَريمًا.
Allah made Musa a noble prophet.
دَعا موسى فِرْعَوْنَ إِلى عِبادَةِ اللهِ.
Musa called Pharaoh to worship Allah.
لكِنَّ فِرْعَوْنَ تَكَبَّرَ وَرَفَضَ.
But Pharaoh was proud and refused.
خَرَجَ موسى بِقَوْمِهِ لَيْلًا مِنْ مِصْرَ.
Musa left Egypt at night with his people.
لَحِقَ بِهِمْ فِرْعَوْنُ وَجُنودُهُ الغاضِبونَ.
Pharaoh and his angry soldiers chased them.
وَقَفَ القَوْمُ أَمامَ البَحْرِ خائِفينَ.
The people stood before the sea, afraid.
وَثِقَ موسى بِاللهِ وَلَمْ يَخَفْ.
Musa trusted Allah and was not afraid.
أَمَرَ اللهُ موسى فَضَرَبَ البَحْرَ.
Allah told Musa, and he struck the sea.
انْشَقَّ البَحْرُ وَصارَ طَريقًا يابِسًا.
The sea split open and became a dry path.
عَبَرَ موسى وَقَوْمُهُ بِسَلامٍ، وَنَجَوْا.
Musa and his people crossed safely and were saved.
اللهُ يَنْصُرُ المُؤْمِنينَ الصّابِرينَ.
Allah helps the believers who are patient.
What did Pharaoh do when Musa called him to Allah?
Pharaoh was too proud and refused, but Allah's help was with Musa and the believers.
What happened when Musa struck the sea?
By Allah's power the sea split open into a dry path so Musa's people could cross safely.
How were Musa and his people saved?
Allah opened the sea and they crossed safely. Allah always helps patient believers who trust Him.
Prophet Yunus (peace be upon him) and the Whale. Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.
يونُسُ عَلَيْهِ السَّلام نَبِيٌّ لِقَوْمِهِ.
Yunus (peace be upon him) was a prophet to his people.
تَرَكَ قَوْمَهُ وَرَكِبَ سَفينَةً في البَحْرِ.
He left his people and boarded a ship at sea.
هاجَتِ الأَمْواجُ، فَنَزَلَ يونُسُ في الماءِ.
The waves grew rough, and Yunus went into the water.
ابْتَلَعَهُ حوتٌ كَبيرٌ في البَحْرِ.
A big whale swallowed him in the sea.
دَعا يونُسُ رَبَّهُ في الظُّلُماتِ.
Yunus called upon his Lord in the darkness.
غَفَرَ اللهُ لَهُ وَأَخْرَجَهُ سالِمًا.
Allah forgave him and brought him out safely.
إِذا دَعَوْنا اللهَ بِصِدْقٍ أَجابَنا.
When we call Allah sincerely, He answers us.
What swallowed Yunus in the sea?
A big whale swallowed Yunus, but Allah kept him safe inside it.
What did Yunus do inside the whale?
Yunus called upon Allah in the darkness and asked for forgiveness, and Allah heard him.
What lesson does this story teach?
When we call on Allah with a sincere heart, He answers us and helps us, just as He saved Yunus.
Prophet Dawud and Prophet Sulaiman (peace be upon them). Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.
داوُدُ عَلَيْهِ السَّلام نَبِيٌّ وَمَلِكٌ حَكيمٌ.
Dawud (peace be upon him) was a prophet and a wise king.
أَعْطاهُ اللهُ صَوْتًا جَميلًا لِتَسْبيحِهِ.
Allah gave him a beautiful voice to praise Allah.
كانَتِ الجِبالُ وَالطَّيْرُ تُسَبِّحُ مَعَهُ.
The mountains and birds glorified Allah with him.
وَابْنُهُ سُلَيْمانُ عَلَيْهِ السَّلام نَبِيٌّ وَمَلِكٌ.
His son Sulaiman (peace be upon him) was also a prophet and king.
عَلَّمَهُ اللهُ كَلامَ الطَّيْرِ وَالحَيَوانِ.
Allah taught him the speech of birds and animals.
مَرَّ يَوْمًا بِوادٍ فيهِ نَمْلٌ كَثيرٌ.
One day he passed a valley full of ants.
قالَتْ نَمْلَةٌ لِلنَّمْلِ: ادْخُلوا بُيوتَكُمْ.
An ant told the others to go into their homes.
سَمِعَ سُلَيْمانُ النَّمْلَةَ فَابْتَسَمَ وَشَكَرَ اللهَ.
Sulaiman heard the ant, smiled, and thanked Allah.
وَكانَ يَتَحَدَّثُ مَعَ الطُّيورِ مِثْلَ الهُدْهُدِ.
He would also speak with birds, like the hoopoe.
شَكَرَ سُلَيْمانُ اللهَ عَلى كُلِّ نِعْمَةٍ.
Sulaiman thanked Allah for every blessing.
النِّعَمُ مِنَ اللهِ، فَنَشْكُرُهُ دائِمًا.
All blessings are from Allah, so we always thank Him.
What did Allah give Prophet Dawud?
Allah gave Dawud a beautiful voice, and even the mountains and birds praised Allah with him.
What did Sulaiman understand?
Allah taught Sulaiman the speech of birds and animals, a special gift from Allah.
What did Sulaiman do when he heard the ant?
Sulaiman smiled and thanked Allah. He was always grateful for Allah's blessings, and so should we be.
Prophet Isa (peace be upon him). Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.
مَرْيَمُ امْرَأَةٌ صالِحَةٌ طاهِرَةٌ.
Maryam was a good and pure woman.
أَرْسَلَ اللهُ إِلَيْها المَلَكَ جِبْريلَ عَلَيْهِ السَّلام.
Allah sent to her the angel Jibril (peace be upon him).
بَشَّرَها بِطِفْلٍ مِنْ عِنْدِ اللهِ.
He gave her good news of a baby from Allah.
وَلَدَتْ مَرْيَمُ عيسى عَلَيْهِ السَّلام بِمُعْجِزَةٍ.
Maryam gave birth to Isa (peace be upon him) by a miracle.
خَلَقَهُ اللهُ بِلا أَبٍ بِقُدْرَتِهِ.
Allah created him without a father, by His power.
تَكَلَّمَ عيسى وَهُوَ طِفْلٌ صَغيرٌ في المَهْدِ.
Isa spoke while he was a small baby in the cradle.
قالَ إِنَّهُ عَبْدُ اللهِ وَنَبِيُّهُ.
He said that he is a servant of Allah and His prophet.
اللهُ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَديرٌ.
Allah is able to do all things.
Which angel came to Maryam?
Allah sent the angel Jibril to Maryam with the good news of baby Isa.
What amazing thing did baby Isa do?
By Allah's power, baby Isa spoke in the cradle and said he was a servant and prophet of Allah.
What does the birth of Isa show us?
Allah created Isa without a father, showing that Allah has power over all things.
Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) — Al-Amin. Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.
وُلِدَ سَيِّدُنا مُحَمَّدٌ ﷺ في مَكَّةَ.
Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) was born in Makkah.
تُوُفِّيَ أَبوهُ وَأُمُّهُ وَهُوَ صَغيرٌ.
His father and mother passed away when he was young.
رَعاهُ جَدُّهُ ثُمَّ عَمُّهُ بِحُبٍّ.
His grandfather and then his uncle cared for him with love.
عَمِلَ في رَعْيِ الغَنَمِ وَهُوَ فَتًى.
As a young boy he worked shepherding sheep.
كانَ صادِقًا وَأَمينًا مَعَ كُلِّ النّاسِ.
He was truthful and trustworthy with all people.
سَمّاهُ النّاسُ الأَمينَ لِأَمانَتِهِ.
People called him Al-Amin because he was so trustworthy.
كانوا يَتْرُكونَ أَمانَتَهُمْ عِنْدَهُ بِثِقَةٍ.
They would leave their valuables with him, trusting him.
كانَ رَحيمًا بِالفُقَراءِ وَالضُّعَفاءِ.
He was kind to the poor and the weak.
كانَ يُحِبُّ الحَيَوانَ وَلا يُؤْذيهِ.
He loved animals and never hurt them.
أَحَبَّهُ النّاسُ لِأَخْلاقِهِ الجَميلَةِ.
People loved him for his beautiful manners.
الصِّدْقُ وَالأَمانَةُ يَجْعَلانِنا مَحْبوبينَ.
Honesty and trustworthiness make us loved.
What did people call Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him)?
People called him Al-Amin because he was so honest and trustworthy, even before he was a prophet.
How did Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) treat people?
He was truthful, trustworthy, and kind to everyone, including the poor and the weak.
What can we learn from his childhood?
We learn to be honest and trustworthy like the Prophet (peace be upon him), so people love and trust us too.
Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) — The First Revelation. Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.
كانَ سَيِّدُنا مُحَمَّدٌ ﷺ يُحِبُّ التَّفَكُّرَ.
Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) loved to think and reflect.
كانَ يَذْهَبُ إِلى غارِ حِراءَ لِيَتَعَبَّدَ.
He would go to the cave of Hira to worship.
كانَ يَتَفَكَّرُ في اللهِ وَفي خَلْقِهِ.
He would reflect on Allah and His creation.
جاءَهُ المَلَكُ جِبْريلُ عَلَيْهِ السَّلام في الغارِ.
The angel Jibril (peace be upon him) came to him in the cave.
قالَ لَهُ جِبْريلُ: اقْرَأْ.
Jibril said to him, Read.
قالَ مُحَمَّدٌ ﷺ: ما أَنا بِقارِئٍ.
Muhammad (peace be upon him) said, I do not know how to read.
أَعادَ جِبْريلُ القَوْلَ، وَعَلَّمَهُ اللهُ.
Jibril repeated it, and Allah taught him.
نَزَلَتْ أَوَّلُ آياتٍ مِنَ القُرْآنِ الكَريمِ.
The first verses of the Holy Quran came down.
عادَ إِلى بَيْتِهِ وَقَلْبُهُ يَخْفِقُ.
He returned home with his heart beating fast.
طَمْأَنَتْهُ زَوْجَتُهُ خَديجَةُ رَضِيَ اللهُ عَنْها.
His wife Khadijah (may Allah be pleased with her) comforted him.
صارَ مُحَمَّدٌ ﷺ نَبِيَّ اللهِ وَرَسولَهُ.
Muhammad (peace be upon him) became the prophet and messenger of Allah.
بَدَأَ يَدْعو النّاسَ إِلى عِبادَةِ اللهِ.
He began to call people to worship Allah.
القُرْآنُ هُوَ كَلامُ اللهِ وَهِدايَتُنا.
The Quran is the word of Allah and our guidance.
نَقْرَأُ القُرْآنَ وَنَعْمَلُ بِهِ بِحُبٍّ.
We read the Quran and follow it with love.
Where did Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) go to worship and reflect?
He would go to the quiet cave of Hira to reflect on Allah and His creation.
Which angel brought the first revelation?
The angel Jibril came to him in the cave and brought the first verses of the Quran.
What is the Quran?
The Quran is the word of Allah, our guidance. We read it and follow it with love.
Bismillah and Alhamdulillah. Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.
جَلَسَت هَنا لِتَأكُلَ طَعامَها.
Hana sat down to eat her food.
قالَت أُمُّها: قولي بِسمِ اللهِ أَوَّلاً.
Her mother said: say Bismillah first.

قالَت هَنا: بِسمِ اللهِ.
ثُمَّ بَدَأَت تَأكُل.
Hana said Bismillah. Then she began to eat.

كانَ الطَّعامُ لَذيذاً وَصِحِّيّاً.
أَكَلَت بِيَدِها اليُمنى.
The food was tasty and healthy. She ate with her right hand.

بَعدَ الطَّعامِ شَكَرَت اللهَ.
قالَت: الحَمدُ لِلَّه.
After the meal she thanked Allah. She said Alhamdulillah.

اللهُ يُعطينا الطَّعامَ كُلَّ يَوم.
نَحنُ نَشكُرُهُ دائِماً.
Allah gives us food every day. We thank Him always.

تَعَلَّمَت هَنا أَدَباً جَميلاً.
نَبدَأُ وَنَنتَهي بِذِكرِ الله.
Hana learned a beautiful manner. We begin and end by remembering Allah.
What do we say before we start eating?
We say Bismillah to begin in the name of Allah, who gives us our food.
What do we say after we finish our food?
We say Alhamdulillah to thank Allah for the meal we enjoyed.
Why do we thank Allah for our food?
Allah provides our food every day, so being thankful is a kind and good habit.
Water for a Thirsty Dog. Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.

كانَ اليَومُ حارّاً جِدّاً.
مَشى رَجُلٌ في الطَّريق.
The day was very hot. A man walked along the road.

رَأى كَلباً صَغيراً عَطشان.
كانَ يَلهَثُ مِنَ العَطَش.
He saw a small, thirsty dog. It was panting from thirst.

كانَ هُناكَ بِئرٌ فيها ماء.
أَرادَ الرَّجُلُ أَن يُساعِدَه.

مَلَأَ حِذاءَهُ بِالماء.
He climbed down to the well himself. He filled his shoe with water.

أَعطى الكَلبَ ماءً بارِداً.
شَرِبَ الكَلبُ وَفَرِحَ.
He gave the dog cool water. The dog drank and was happy.

الإِسلامُ يُعَلِّمُنا الرَّحمَة.
نَكونُ رُحَماءَ بِالحَيَوانات.
Islam teaches us mercy. We are kind to animals.

اللهُ يُحِبُّ العَمَلَ الطَّيِّب.
كُلُّ لُطفٍ لَهُ أَجرٌ كَبير.
Allah loves good deeds. Every act of kindness has a great reward.
What did the man do for the thirsty dog?
The man kindly gave the dog water, and Allah loves such gentle deeds.
How does Islam teach us to treat animals?
Islam teaches us to be merciful to every creature, even a small animal.
What happens when we are kind to animals?
Allah loves kindness, so caring for animals brings us reward and joy.
A Smile is Charity. Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.

ذَهَبَت هَنا إِلى المَدرَسَة.
رَأَت صَديقَةً حَزينَة.
Hana went to school. She saw a sad friend.

جَلَسَت الصَّديقَةُ وَحدَها.
لَم تَلعَب مَعَ أَحَد.
The friend sat all alone. She did not play with anyone.

اِبتَسَمَت هَنا لَها بِلُطف.
قالَت: تَعالي نَلعَبُ مَعاً.
Hana smiled at her kindly. She said: come, let us play together.

فَرِحَت الصَّديقَةُ كَثيراً.
عادَتِ البَسمَةُ إِلى وَجهِها.
The friend became very happy. The smile returned to her face.

الاِبتِسامَةُ عَمَلٌ جَميل.
هِيَ صَدَقَةٌ وَخَير.
A smile is a beautiful thing. It is charity and goodness.

نُدخِلُ الفَرَحَ في القُلوب.
بِوَجهٍ لَطيفٍ وَبَسمَة.
We bring joy into hearts with a kind face and a smile.

تَعَلَّمَت هَنا دَرساً جَميلاً.
البَسمَةُ تَنشُرُ السَّعادَة.
Hana learned a lovely lesson. A smile spreads happiness.
What did Hana do when she saw a sad friend?
Hana's kind smile cheered her friend up, and a smile is a form of charity.
Why is a smile called a charity?
A gentle smile makes others happy, so it counts as a good and giving deed.
How should we treat someone who feels sad?
Being cheerful and kind helps sad people feel welcome and loved.
Love and Respect for Parents. Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.

كانَت هَنا في البَيتِ تَلعَب.
نادَتها أُمُّها بِلُطف.
Hana was at home playing. Her mother called her gently.

أَجابَت هَنا بِسُرعَة.
قالَت: نَعَم يا أُمّي.
Hana answered quickly. She said: yes, mother.

قالَت الأُمُّ: أَنا مُتعَبَة.
هَل تُساعِدينَني قَليلاً؟
Her mother said: I am tired. Will you help me a little?

قالَت هَنا: بِكُلِّ سُرور.
أُحِبُّ أَن أُساعِدَكِ.
Hana said: with pleasure. I love to help you.

رَتَّبَت الغُرفَةَ بِنَفسِها.
ثُمَّ حَمَلَت الأَطباق.
She tidied the room herself. Then she carried the plates.

أَحضَرَت لِأُمِّها كوبَ ماء.
قالَت: اِستَريحي يا أُمّي.
She brought her mother a cup of water. She said: rest, mother.

اِبتَسَمَتِ الأُمُّ وَدَعَت لَها.
قالَت: بارَكَ اللهُ فيكِ.
Her mother smiled and prayed for her. She said: may Allah bless you.

بِرُّ الوالِدَينِ مِنَ الإِيمان.
نُطيعُهُما وَنُحِبُّهُما.
Kindness to parents is part of faith. We obey them and love them.

اللهُ أَمَرَنا بِالإِحسانِ إِلَيهِما.
نَتَكَلَّمُ مَعَهُما بِلُطف.
Allah commanded us to be good to them. We speak to them gently.

تَعَلَّمَت هَنا دَرساً غالِياً.
طاعَةُ الوالِدَينِ خَيرٌ كَبير.
Hana learned a precious lesson. Obeying our parents is a great good.
What did Hana do when her tired mother asked for help?
Hana helped her mother with a happy heart, which is beautiful kindness to parents.
How should we speak to our parents?
Allah tells us to speak kindly and softly to our parents at all times.
Why do we honour our mother and father?
Being good to parents is part of our faith and pleases Allah greatly.
Always Tell the Truth. Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.

لَعِبَ الأَولادُ بِالكُرَة.
كانَت هَنا مَعَهُم.
The children played with a ball. Hana was with them.

طارَتِ الكُرَةُ بِقُوَّة.
كَسَرَت إِناءَ الأَزهار.
The ball flew hard. It broke the flower pot.

خافَ الأَطفالُ وَهَرَبوا.
لَكِنَّ هَنا وَقَفَت.
The children were scared and ran. But Hana stood still.

جاءَت أُمُّها وَسَأَلَت.
قالَت هَنا: أَنا كَسَرتُه.
Her mother came and asked. Hana said: I broke it.

لَم تَكذِب هَنا أَبَداً.
قالَتِ الحَقيقَةَ بِشَجاعَة.
Hana did not lie at all. She told the truth bravely.

اِبتَسَمَتِ الأُمُّ وَقالَت:
أَنا فَخورَةٌ بِصِدقِكِ.
Her mother smiled and said: I am proud of your honesty.

الصِّدقُ خُلُقٌ جَميل.
اللهُ يُحِبُّ الصّادِقين.
Honesty is a beautiful character. Allah loves those who are truthful.
What did Hana do after the pot broke?
Hana bravely told the truth, and honesty is a trait Allah loves.
How did her mother feel about Hana's honesty?
Telling the truth made her mother proud, because honesty is very precious.
Why should we always tell the truth?
Being truthful builds trust and pleases Allah, even when it is hard.
Spreading Peace. Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.

دَخَلَت هَنا إِلى البَيت.
قالَت: السَّلامُ عَلَيكُم.
Hana entered the house. She said the greeting of peace.

رَدَّت عَلَيها العائِلَة.
وَعَلَيكُمُ السَّلام.
Her family replied to her with the same peaceful greeting.

رَأَت هَنا جارَتَها.
حَيَّتها بِالسَّلامِ أَيضاً.
Hana saw her neighbour. She greeted her with peace too.

السَّلامُ يَعني الأَمانَ.
نَدعو لِبَعضِنا بِالخَير.
The greeting means safety. We wish each other well.

نُلقي السَّلامَ عَلى الجَميع.
مَن نَعرِفُ وَمَن لا نَعرِف.
We greet everyone, those we know and those we do not know.

السَّلامُ يَجمَعُ القُلوب.
وَيَنشُرُ المَحَبَّةَ بَينَنا.
The greeting brings hearts together and spreads love among us.

تَعَلَّمَت هَنا عادَةً جَميلَة.
نَبدَأُ كَلامَنا بِالسَّلام.
Hana learned a lovely habit. We begin our talk with peace.
What did Hana say when she entered the house?
Greeting others with peace is a beautiful Muslim habit that spreads love.
What does the greeting of peace wish for others?
The peace greeting is a kind prayer that others be safe and well.
Who should we greet with peace?
We greet everyone kindly, whether we know them or not, to spread peace.
Sharing with a Neighbour. Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.

طَبَخَت أُمُّ هَنا طَعاماً لَذيذاً.
مَلَأَتِ البَيتَ رائِحَةٌ طَيِّبَة.
Hana's mother cooked delicious food. A lovely smell filled the house.

قالَتِ الأُمُّ: عِندَنا كَثير.
لِنُشارِك جيرانَنا الطَّعام.
Her mother said: we have plenty. Let us share the food with our neighbours.

مَلَأَت هَنا صَحناً كَبيراً.
حَمَلَتهُ بِيَدَيها بِحَذَر.
Hana filled a big plate. She carried it carefully in her hands.

طَرَقَت بابَ الجيران.
قالَت: هَذا لَكُم بِمَحَبَّة.
She knocked on the neighbour's door. She said: this is for you with love.

فَرِحَتِ الجارَةُ كَثيراً.
شَكَرَت هَنا وَدَعَت لَها.
The neighbour was very happy. She thanked Hana and prayed for her.

الجارُ لَهُ حَقٌّ عَلَينا.
نُحسِنُ إِلَيهِ وَنُشارِكُه.
The neighbour has a right upon us. We are good to him and share with him.

تَعَلَّمَت هَنا خَيراً كَثيراً.
المُشارَكَةُ تَزيدُ المَحَبَّة.
Hana learned much good. Sharing increases love between people.
What did Hana do with the extra food?
Hana kindly shared food with her neighbour, which is a good and generous act.
How did the neighbour feel about the gift?
Sharing food made the neighbour happy and thankful, spreading kindness.
Why do we share with our neighbours?
Islam teaches that neighbours deserve our kindness, so sharing brings great reward.
Cleanliness is Part of Faith. Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.

اِستَيقَظَت هَنا في الصَّباح.
غَسَلَت وَجهَها وَيَدَيها.
Hana woke up in the morning. She washed her face and hands.

نَظَّفَت أَسنانَها جَيِّداً.
صارَت رائِحَتُها طَيِّبَة.
She cleaned her teeth well. Her breath became fresh and nice.

لَبِسَت ثِياباً نَظيفَة.
مَشَّطَت شَعرَها بِعِنايَة.
She wore clean clothes. She combed her hair with care.

رَتَّبَت غُرفَتَها بِنَفسِها.
وَضَعَت كُلَّ شَيءٍ في مَكانِه.
She tidied her room herself. She put everything in its place.

غَسَلَت يَدَيها قَبلَ الطَّعام.
وَبَعدَهُ أَيضاً.
She washed her hands before eating, and afterwards too.

النَّظافَةُ مِنَ الإِيمان.
اللهُ يُحِبُّ الطّاهِرينَ النِّظاف.
Cleanliness is part of faith. Allah loves those who are pure and clean.

تَعَلَّمَت هَنا عادَةً جَميلَة.
نَحُنُ نَظيفونَ كُلَّ يَوم.
Hana learned a lovely habit. We stay clean every day.
What did Hana do in the morning?
Hana kept herself clean, and staying clean is a beautiful part of faith.
When should we wash our hands?
Washing hands before and after food keeps us clean and healthy, which pleases Allah.
Why do Muslims try to stay clean?
Islam teaches that cleanliness is part of faith, and Allah loves those who stay pure.
Being Patient. Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.

زَرَعَت هَنا بَذرَةً صَغيرَة.
أَرادَت زَهرَةً جَميلَة.
Hana planted a tiny seed. She wanted a beautiful flower.

سَقَتِ البَذرَةَ كُلَّ يَوم.
لَكِنَّها لَم تَنبُت بَعد.
She watered the seed every day. But it did not grow yet.

حَزِنَت هَنا قَليلاً.
قالَت: مَتى تَكبُرُ الزَّهرَة؟
Hana felt a little sad. She said: when will the flower grow?

قالَت أُمُّها: اِصبِري يا هَنا.
كُلُّ شَيءٍ يَحتاجُ وَقتاً.
Her mother said: be patient, Hana. Everything needs time.

اِنتَظَرَت هَنا بِصَبرٍ جَميل.
وَاصَلَتِ السَّقيَ كُلَّ يَوم.
Hana waited with lovely patience. She kept watering every day.

بَعدَ أَيّامٍ نَبَتَتِ الزَّهرَة.
صارَت جَميلَةً وَمُلَوَّنَة.
After some days the flower grew. It became lovely and colourful.

فَرِحَت هَنا فَرَحاً كَبيراً.
صَبرُها أَعطاها ثَمَرَةً حُلوَة.
Hana was filled with joy. Her patience gave her a sweet result.

الصَّبرُ خُلُقٌ عَظيم.
اللهُ مَعَ الصّابِرين.
Patience is a great character. Allah is with those who are patient.

عِندَ الصُّعوبَةِ نَصبِر.
وَنَثِقُ بِأَنَّ اللهَ يُعين.
When things are hard we stay patient. We trust that Allah helps us.

تَعَلَّمَت هَنا دَرساً غالِياً.
الصَّبرُ مِفتاحُ الفَرَج.
Hana learned a precious lesson. Patience is the key to relief.
What did Hana do while she waited for the flower?
Hana's patience and care helped her flower grow, and Allah loves patient people.
What did her patience give her in the end?
Waiting patiently brought Hana a lovely flower, showing good things take time.
What should we do when things feel hard?
When life is difficult, patience and trust in Allah help us find relief.
Saying Insha'Allah. Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.

قالَت هَنا: غَداً سَأَزورُ جَدَّتي.
أَنا مُتَحَمِّسَةٌ جِدّاً.
Hana said: tomorrow I will visit my grandmother. I am very excited.

قالَت أُمُّها: قولي إِن شاءَ الله.
كُلُّ شَيءٍ بِيَدِ الله.
Her mother said: say Insha'Allah. Everything is in Allah's hands.

قالَت هَنا: سَأَزورُها إِن شاءَ الله.
إِذا أَرادَ اللهُ ذَلِك.
Hana said: I will visit her Insha'Allah, if Allah wills it.

نَحنُ نُخَطِّطُ لِلأُمور.
لَكِنَّ اللهَ هُوَ المُدَبِّر.
We make our plans, but Allah is the one who arranges everything.

نَقولُ إِن شاءَ الله بِثِقَة.
وَنَتَوَكَّلُ عَلى الله.
We say Insha'Allah with confidence. We place our trust in Allah.

في الغَدِ زارَت جَدَّتَها.
وَفَرِحَتِ العائِلَةُ كُلُّها.
The next day she visited her grandmother. The whole family was happy.

تَعَلَّمَت هَنا عادَةً طَيِّبَة.
نَذكُرُ اللهَ في كُلِّ خُطَّة.
Hana learned a good habit. We remember Allah in every plan.
What did Hana say about her plan for tomorrow?
Saying Insha'Allah reminds us that our plans happen only if Allah wills.
Who is truly in charge of what happens?
We plan, but Allah arranges all things, so we trust Him with our future.
Why do we say Insha'Allah about our plans?
Saying Insha'Allah shows we make plans while trusting Allah to guide the outcome.
The Reward of Ramadan. Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.

جاءَ شَهرُ رَمَضانَ المُبارَك.
فَرِحَت هَنا كَثيراً.
The blessed month of Ramadan came. Hana was very happy.

أَكَلَتِ العائِلَةُ قَبلَ الفَجر.
هَذا يُسَمّى السُّحور.
The family ate before dawn. This meal is called suhoor.

في النَّهارِ نَصومُ عَنِ الطَّعام.
وَعَنِ الشَّرابِ أَيضاً.
In the day we fast from food, and from drink as well.

الصِّيامُ يُعَلِّمُنا الصَّبر.
وَيُذَكِّرُنا بِالفُقَراء.
Fasting teaches us patience. It reminds us of the poor.

في رَمَضانَ نَكونُ لُطَفاء.
نُساعِدُ وَنُشارِكُ الطَّعام.
In Ramadan we are kind. We help others and share our food.

عِندَ الغُروبِ نُفطِر.
نَأكُلُ التَّمرَ وَنَشرَبُ الماء.
At sunset we break our fast. We eat dates and drink water.

نَقرَأُ القُرآنَ في اللَّيل.
وَنَدعو اللهَ كَثيراً.
We read the Quran at night. We pray to Allah a lot.

رَمَضانُ شَهرُ الخَيرِ وَالبَرَكَة.
اللهُ يُضاعِفُ الأَجرَ فيه.
Ramadan is a month of goodness and blessing. Allah multiplies the reward in it.

صامَت هَنا بِفَرَحٍ وَحُب.
وَتَصَدَّقَت عَلى المَساكين.
Hana fasted with joy and love. She gave charity to those in need.

تَعَلَّمَت هَنا مَعنى رَمَضان.
صِيامٌ وَصَبرٌ وَكَرَم.
Hana learned the meaning of Ramadan: fasting, patience, and generosity.
What do Muslims do during the day in Ramadan?
Fasting in Ramadan teaches patience and reminds us of people who have little.
What kind acts do we do in Ramadan?
Ramadan is a time to be generous, sharing food and helping those in need.
Why is Ramadan a special month?
Ramadan is full of blessing, and Allah rewards our good deeds even more in it.
The Joy of Eid. Let's read! Listen to each page, follow the Arabic, and read along out loud.

اِنتَهى شَهرُ رَمَضان.
جاءَ يَومُ العيدِ السَّعيد.
The month of Ramadan ended. The happy day of Eid arrived.

لَبِسَت هَنا ثِياباً جَديدَة.
كانَت جَميلَةً وَنَظيفَة.
Hana wore new clothes. They were beautiful and clean.

ذَهَبَتِ العائِلَةُ لِصَلاةِ العيد.
كانَ النّاسُ سُعَداءَ كُلُّهُم.
The family went to the Eid prayer. All the people were happy.

قالَتِ العائِلَةُ: عيدٌ مُبارَك.
وَعانَقَ بَعضُهُم بَعضاً.
The family said: blessed Eid. They hugged one another warmly.

سامَحَت هَنا صَديقَتَها.
نَسِيَتِ الخِلافَ القَديم.
Hana forgave her friend. She forgot the old disagreement.

أَعطَتِ العائِلَةُ الهَدايا.
وَتَصَدَّقوا عَلى الفُقَراء.
The family gave gifts. They also gave charity to the poor.

وَزَّعَت هَنا الحَلوى.
عَلى الأَطفالِ وَالجيران.
Hana handed out sweets to the children and the neighbours.

العيدُ يَومُ فَرَحٍ وَمَحَبَّة.
نَعفو وَنُعطي وَنَبتَسِم.
Eid is a day of joy and love. We forgive, we give, and we smile.

نَزورُ الأَهلَ وَالأَصدِقاء.
وَنُدخِلُ السُّرورَ في القُلوب.
We visit family and friends. We bring happiness into hearts.

تَعَلَّمَت هَنا مَعنى العيد.
عَفوٌ وَعَطاءٌ وَفَرَح.
Hana learned the meaning of Eid: forgiveness, giving, and joy.
What did Hana do with her friend on Eid?
Eid is a time to forgive others and let go of old arguments with a kind heart.
What did the family do for the poor on Eid?
Giving to those in need is a beautiful part of Eid, spreading joy to everyone.
What is the meaning of Eid?
Eid teaches us to forgive, give generously, and share happiness with all.
